faqs.question: What's the difference between wasps, yellow jackets, and hornets?
faqs.answer: While all are stinging insects, wasps have slender bodies and build open-comb nests, yellow jackets are aggressive ground-nesters with bright markings, and hornets are larger with enclosed aerial nests. Our North Bellmore technicians are trained to identify and safely remove all stinging insect species common to Long Island.
